title: Agentic Operating Model Best Practice
summary: Defines best-in-class agentic AI as a governed operating model with retrieval, planning, tools, verification, human control, and feedback loops.
claims: Best-in-class agentic AI is an operating model, not just a model connected to tools.
The critical layers are intent framing, context retrieval, reasoning and planning, tool execution, verification, human control, and feedback loops.
Retrieval before fluency, verification before autonomy, workflow before feature, and governance before scale are the core operating rules.
proof_points: This structure matches the retrieval-first, validation-aware direction already used in the Joz backend.
It aligns with Joz's broader principles of systems before features, decision quality, and appropriate autonomy.
It gives a business-safe explanation for how agentic AI becomes operationally credible.
